sentence_reviews.text: Discrete units convert text, code, actions, images, or audio into sequences a model can consume.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: preprocessing discrete input/text data, language models, word embeddings, and vector quantization (lines 18-20, 623-645, and 655-659); AIMA contents.html: language models, lexicon/grammar, word representations, and Appendix B languages/algorithms (lines 621-660 and 776-780).
sentence_reviews.notes: Text/token-like discrete preprocessing and language models are supported. Code, actions, images, and audio are not directly supported as tokenized inputs in the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units is to connect Information theory, Discrete mathematics & logic to downstream capabilities including Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Self-supervised learning, Masked prediction objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: information theory, preprocessing discrete/text data, self-supervised learning, and unsupervised pretraining (lines 179-205, 18-20, and 585-587); AIMA contents.html: language models, word representations, pretrained contextual representations, and masked language models (lines 621-660).
sentence_reviews.notes: Information theory, discrete text preprocessing, self-supervised learning, and masked language models are supported. Multimodal/simulated/streaming data and the broad symbolic-units phrasing are only partially supported.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: common image datasets, common text datasets, and preprocessing discrete/text data (lines 15-20).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports discrete/text preprocessing and image/text datasets, but not tokens as a substrate for multimodal, simulated, or streaming data.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: self-supervised learning and unsupervised pretraining (lines 9-13 and 585-587); AIMA contents.html: pretrained word embeddings/contextual representations and masked language models (lines 655-659).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support self-supervised/pretraining contexts with language tokens/representations. 'Substrate for' is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Chapter 24.5 'Pretraining and Transfer Learning', including pretrained contextual representations and masked language models (lines 655-659).
sentence_reviews.notes: Masked language models directly support a masked-prediction connection for tokens, though the exact objective wording is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Information theory, Discrete mathematics & logic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: preprocessing discrete/text data, information theory, and vector quantization (lines 18-20, 179-205, and 655-659); AIMA contents.html: fairness/bias and formal languages/algorithms (lines 745-749 and 776-780).
sentence_reviews.notes: Information theory and discrete/formal language context are supported. Information loss, invariance difficulty, and tokenization-specific bias are not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: information theory chapter including entropy, cross entropy, KL divergence, mutual information, and perplexity (lines 179-205).
sentence_reviews.notes: Information-theoretic quantities are directly relevant to discrete symbol modeling and language-model evaluation, but the rationale wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Discrete mathematics & logic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: language models, grammar/lexicon, word representations, and Appendix B defining languages with BNF (lines 621-660 and 776-780); PML book1 long TOC: preprocessing discrete input data and text data (lines 18-20).
sentence_reviews.notes: Discrete text and formal-language material support the dependency; logic is not directly emphasized in the inspected headings.

sentence_reviews.text: Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Programming languages, compilers & type systems.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Appendix B 'Notes on Languages and Algorithms', including Backus--Naur Form and pseudocode (lines 776-780).
sentence_reviews.notes: Formal language notation is supported, but compilers and type systems are not directly supported by the inspected source.

edge_reviews.notes: Entropy, cross entropy, KL, mutual information, and perplexity support the information-theory dependency for token/vocabulary modeling.
Discrete preprocessing, grammar/lexicon, and formal language material make the requires/core edge defensible.
Formal languages and pseudocode are relevant, but the inspected evidence does not establish compilers/type systems as a dependency. Common strength is plausible but weakly supported.
